title: Prevent Non-Privileged Users from Executing Privileged Functions and Capture Execution in Audit Logs
description: prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs

checks.name: Privileged Functions are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that privileged functions are defined by confirming elevated user rights assignments and local Administrators group membership are scoped to named accounts only

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that non-privileged users are defined by confirming standard Users group membership is distinct from Administrators and no standard user holds elevated rights

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Prevented from Executing Privileged Functions
checks.purpose: Confirm that UAC, AppLocker or Software Restriction Policies, and user rights assignments actively prevent standard users from executing privileged functions

checks.name: Execution of Privileged Functions is Captured in Audit Logs
checks.purpose: Verify that Windows audit policy captures privilege use, account management, and policy change events so privileged function execution is recorded in audit logs

checks.name: Privileged Functions are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that privileged functions are defined by confirming Domain Admins, Schema Admins, and other high-privilege AD group memberships are scoped to named accounts only

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that non-privileged users are defined by confirming DA accounts are not used for daily tasks and standard users are explicitly separated from privileged identities

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Prevented from Executing Privileged Functions
checks.purpose: Confirm that AD group restrictions, UAC enforcement, and user rights assignments actively prevent standard users from executing privileged functions on the server

checks.name: Execution of Privileged Functions is Captured in Audit Logs
checks.purpose: Verify that Windows advanced audit policy captures privilege use, account management, policy change, and sensitive privilege use events on the server

checks.name: Privileged Functions are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of privileged functions on Linux is a GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of non-privileged users on Linux is a GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Prevented from Executing Privileged Functions
checks.purpose: Confirm that sudoers restrictions, PAM controls, SELinux/AppArmor enforcement, and file permissions actively prevent standard users from executing privileged functions

checks.name: Execution of Privileged Functions is Captured in Audit Logs
checks.purpose: Verify that auditd is active with rules capturing sudo usage, privilege escalation attempts, and sensitive file access so privileged function execution is recorded

checks.name: Privileged Functions are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of privileged functions on Debian is a GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of non-privileged users on Debian is a GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: Non-Privileged Users are Prevented from Executing Privileged Functions
checks.purpose: Confirm that sudoers restrictions, PAM controls, AppArmor enforcement, and file permissions actively prevent standard users from executing privileged functions

checks.name: Execution of Privileged Functions is Captured in Audit Logs
checks.purpose: Verify that auditd is active with rules capturing sudo usage, privilege escalation attempts, and sensitive file access so privileged function execution is recorded

remediation: Configure User Account Control on Windows to require administrator credentials for all privileged operations and set it to always notify. Use Group Policy to restrict elevation rights and user rights assignments to designated administrative accounts only. On Linux, configure sudoers to restrict which users can run privileged commands and enable auditd with rules that capture sudo usage and privilege escalation attempts. Enable Windows audit policy for Privilege Use, Account Management, and Policy Change categories so that all privileged function execution is recorded in the Security event log.
